# apsum_rect_ann_exact { #astroapers.apsum_rect_ann_exact }

```python
apsum_rect_ann_exact(
    data,
    x,
    y,
    w_in,
    h_in,
    w_out,
    h_out,
    theta_in=0.0,
    theta_out=None,
    *,
    mask=None,
    return_npix=True,
    validate=True,
)
```

Return exact rotated-rectangle-annulus aperture sums for one or many aperture centers.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**data**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Two-dimensional image. For maximum performance with raw contiguous arrays, use ``import astroapers._rust as aapr`` and call the raw functions directly.

<code>[**x, y**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [scalar or array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Aperture center coordinates in pixel units. Inputs are converted to contiguous ``float64`` arrays. Shapes must match after ``numpy.atleast_1d``. The return shape matches that broadcast-free input shape, so scalar inputs return one-element arrays.

<code>[**w_in, h_in**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Inner rectangle full width and height in pixels. ``w_in`` is measured along the inner rectangle's local x axis, and ``h_in`` along its local y axis.

<code>[**w_out, h_out**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Outer rectangle full width and height in pixels. ``w_out`` is measured along the outer rectangle's local x axis, and ``h_out`` along its local y axis. Each outer dimension must be larger than the corresponding inner dimension. At ``theta_out=0``, the outer width axis is aligned with the image x axis and the outer height axis with the image y axis.

<code>[**theta_in**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Inner rectangle rotation angle in radians, measured counterclockwise from the positive image x axis to the inner rectangle's local width axis.

<code>[**theta_out**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float or None, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Outer rectangle rotation angle in radians, measured counterclockwise from the positive image x axis to the outer rectangle's local width axis. If `None`, uses ``theta_in``.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with the same shape as ``data``. ``True`` pixels are excluded from both the aperture sum and effective pixel count. Values are converted to boolean.

<code>[**return_npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `True`, return ``(apsum, npix)``. If `False`, return only ``apsum``.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**apsum**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sum of the unmasked data values multiplied by the aperture weights.

<code>[**npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Effective in-frame pixel count, returned only when ``return_npix=True``. This is the sum of aperture weights after image clipping and mask exclusion.

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

For matched inner and outer angles, this uses outer-minus-inner direct aperture summation. For split-angle annuli, it uses Rust-generated bbox-tight annulus weights and the same ``BoundingBox.apsum`` reduction.
